lead.name: Dr. Satish Kumar Regonda
lead.title: Associate Professor
departments: Department of Civil Engineering — Hydraulics & Water Resources Engineering
lead.office: Room B-212, Department of Civil Engineering, IIT Hyderabad, Kandi, Sangareddy, Telangana, India
bio: Dr. Satish Kumar Regonda is an Associate Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering (Hydraulics & Water Resources Engineering) and the Department of Climate Change at IIT Hyderabad, where he leads RAFT.

His research spans urban and riverine flood modelling, flood and seasonal water-supply forecasting, hydroclimatology, and the application of statistics, data science and machine learning to water and climate problems.

Before joining IIT Hyderabad in 2017, he spent more than a decade in the United States — as a research hydrologist with NOAA’s National Weather Service, a hydroclimate consultant at the World Bank, and a research scientist at Johns Hopkins University — contributing to operational forecasting systems. He earned his PhD from the University of Colorado Boulder.

education.institution: Kakatiya Institute of Technology & Sciences, Warangal

positions: Head, Department of Climate Change, IIT Hyderabad — 2019–2022
Hydroclimate Consultant, World Bank, Washington DC — 2014–2016
Associate Research Scientist, Johns Hopkins University — 2015–2016
Research Hydrologist, US National Weather Service / NOAA — 2007–2013
